title: breynia oblongifolia var. suborbicularis
intro: Breynia oblongifolia var. suborbicularis, also known as the 'Screwpine', is a variety of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Breynia oblongifolia var. suborbicularis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
